reasoning: Initial observation shows V = 20.0 when I = 2.0 and R = 10.0. To determine the relationship, I will first increase I to see if V changes.
Increasing I from 2.0 to 3.0 resulted in V increasing from 20.0 to 30.0 while R remained constant. This suggests V is directly proportional to I. To further confirm, I will now decrease I.
Decreasing I from 3.0 to 2.0 returned V to its original value of 20.0, confirming that V is directly proportional to I. Now I will increase R to observe its effect.

Increasing R from 10.0 to 20.0 while I remained constant increased V from 20.0 to 40.0. This suggests that V is also directly proportional to R. Given that V is directly proportional to both I and R, and the initial values show V = 40.0 when I = 2.0 and R = 20.0, the equation appears to be V = I * R.
